$comment: User-level git CLI guardrails. First-match-wins rule list. Default posture: ALLOW unmatched. Project directive: 'Never rewrite git history' — history-rewriting commands are DENY (not ASK). Loaded by Gate-ShellCommands.ps1 at preToolUse time.

rules.$comment: DENY: force-push to protected branches — destroys shared history
rules.reason: BLOCKED: Force-push to protected branch rewrites shared history (project directive: never rewrite git history)

rules.$comment: DENY: force-push to protected branches (branch before flag)
rules.reason: BLOCKED: Force-push to protected branch rewrites shared history (project directive: never rewrite git history)

rules.$comment: DENY: delete protected branches on remote
rules.reason: BLOCKED: Deleting protected branch on remote (project directive: never rewrite git history)

rules.$comment: DENY: delete protected branches on remote (colon syntax)
rules.reason: BLOCKED: Deleting protected branch on remote via :branch syntax (project directive: never rewrite git history)
BLOCKED: git filter-branch rewrites repository history (project directive: never rewrite git history)

rules.$comment: DENY: git-filter-repo — newer bulk history rewrite tool
rules.reason: BLOCKED: git filter-repo rewrites repository history (project directive: never rewrite git history)

rules.$comment: DENY: reflog expire --expire=now --all — destroys recovery safety net
rules.reason: BLOCKED: Expiring reflog destroys the recovery safety net (project directive: never rewrite git history)

rules.$comment: DENY: gc --prune=now — destroys unreachable objects immediately
rules.reason: BLOCKED: gc --prune=now destroys unreachable objects immediately, removing recovery options

rules.$comment: DENY: update-ref -d on protected branches — direct ref deletion
rules.reason: BLOCKED: Direct ref deletion on protected branch (project directive: never rewrite git history)

rules.$comment: DENY: push --mirror — overwrites ALL remote refs
rules.reason: BLOCKED: push --mirror overwrites all remote refs including protected branches

rules.$comment: DENY: bare force-push with no explicit branch (DEF-8 — target unknown, fail safe)
rules.reason: BLOCKED: bare force-push with no branch specified — cannot confirm target is non-protected

rules.$comment: ALLOW: read-only commands — short-circuit before ASK rules (branch/tag/remote removed: their destructive variants must reach ASK rules below)
rules.reason: Read-only operation
Read-only config lookup
Read-only config listing
Staging is fully reversible
Normal commit — no history rewrite

rules.$comment: ASK: git push without force/delete/mirror — user directive: every push asks, no silent ALLOW (\b handles bare 'git push' with no args)
rules.reason: Confirm before pushing to remote (user directive: every push asks)
Normal pull
Fetch is read-only

rules.$comment: ALLOW: git checkout/switch to a branch (DEF-4: exclude dot and dot-prefix restore patterns)
rules.reason: Branch switch is safe
Normal merge
Stashing is reversible
Stash restore/inspect is safe
Tag creation is safe
Repository initialization/cloning is safe
Informational command
Aborting a merge is safe
Aborting a rebase is safe
Force-push can overwrite remote history — confirm target branch

rules.$comment: ASK: git reset --hard (discards working changes and commits)
rules.reason: Hard reset discards uncommitted changes and can lose commits
git clean permanently removes untracked files
Discards uncommitted changes to specified files

rules.$comment: ASK: git checkout . (discard all working changes)
rules.reason: Discards all uncommitted working tree changes
Restoring worktree discards uncommitted changes
Force-deleting a branch may lose unmerged commits

rules.$comment: ASK: git branch -d (non-force delete — DEF-1: was bypassed by read-only ALLOW; branch removed from that pattern)
rules.reason: Deleting a merged branch removes the local ref — confirm intent

rules.$comment: ASK: git branch -m/-M (rename or force-rename — DEF-1: was bypassed by read-only ALLOW)
rules.reason: Renaming a branch (-M force-renames, discarding the target if it exists)
Deleting a tag removes a named reference

rules.$comment: ASK: git push --delete tags (remote tag deletion)
rules.reason: Deleting remote tags affects release references

rules.$comment: ASK: git rebase (interactive or otherwise — rewrites local history)
rules.reason: Rebase rewrites commit history (confirm scope)
Cherry-pick duplicates commits and may cause conflicts

rules.$comment: ASK: git stash drop/clear (destroys stashed changes)
rules.reason: Dropping/clearing stash permanently discards saved changes

rules.$comment: ASK: git remote remove/rm (removes remote config)
rules.reason: Removing a remote deletes its tracking configuration
Modifying global git config affects all repositories
Deinitializing a submodule removes its working tree
Removing a worktree deletes its working tree
Amending rewrites the last commit (history modification)

rules.$comment: ASK: git revert (creates reversal commit — confirm intent)
rules.reason: Revert creates a new commit undoing changes — confirm intent

rules.$comment: ASK: git merge --strategy=ours (silently discards incoming changes)
rules.reason: Merge strategy 'ours' silently discards all incoming changes

rules.$comment: ASK: git push --delete (non-protected branch deletion on remote)
rules.reason: Deleting a remote branch — confirm intent
